title: Hash Table — When to Resize
description:
A hash table starts with capacity c and resizes (doubles capacity) when the load factor (size / capacity) exceeds 2/3. Given n insertions (all unique keys), determine:
- How many resizes happen
- The final capacity after all insertions
inputSpec: First line: initial capacity c. Second line: n (number of insertions).
outputSpec: Two integers: number of resizes and final capacity.

botCodes.code:
c=int(input())
n=int(input())
resizes=0
for i in range(1,n+1):
    if i/c>2/3:
        c*=2;resizes+=1
print(resizes,c)

#include<stdio.h>
int main(){int c;long long n;scanf("%d %lld",&c,&n);int r=0;for(long long i=1;i<=n;i++){if(3*i>2*c){c*=2;r++;}}printf("%d %d\n",r,c);}
let()=let c=ref(Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id) and n=Scanf.scanf" %d" Fun.id in let r=ref 0 in for i=1 to n do if 3*i>2* !c then begin c:= !c*2;incr r end done;Printf.printf"%d %d\n" !r !c
